mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically increasing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-cut periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points within a single month signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in a month, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 52% over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in housing starts and real GDP expansion confirms a broad-based demand recovery, which lifts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample windows, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds the CPI inflation rate, real rates turn positive and compress equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly negative when real rates are positive, or the signal's predictive power vanishes during periods of yield curve inversion.

mechanism_narrative: An upward revision in the industrial production index reflects stronger manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, which supports corporate profit margins and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Rank IC for INDPRO month-over-month changes remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistically significant difference between positive and negative INDPRO print periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales exceeding 2.0 percent indicates a sharp contraction in consumer spending, which reduces near-term earnings guidance and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward directional accuracy falls below 50% when RSAFS drops > 2.0%, or forward returns in contraction months are not statistically lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a self-reinforcing labor market expansion, which sustains wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to outperform a random baseline in more than 40% of 60-day forward windows, or the interaction effect reverses sign during high-inflation environments.

mechanism_narrative: During periods where housing starts outpace industrial production growth, demand shifts toward interest-rate-sensitive sectors, causing a relative rotation that depresses broad equity index returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly negative when HOUST growth exceeds INDPRO growth, or the signal's predictive power disappears during financial crisis periods.
